scope: Implemented Rust core provider abstraction, engine orchestration, deterministic mock provider, postprocessing pipeline, and fake-tested pi subprocess provider. No HTTP endpoints, editor clients, or real pi smoke tests were implemented or run.
summary: Added crates/autocomplete-core with a dyn-safe async CompletionProvider trait, AutocompleteEngine, typed provider errors, deterministic MockProvider, and default postprocessors.
Added postprocessors for newline normalization, markdown fence stripping, repeated-prefix removal, max-character truncation, and explanation/prose rejection.

Added crates/autocomplete-provider-pi with strict prompt rendering, pi subprocess command construction, JSONL assistant-event extraction, provider-response JSON parsing, one malformed-output repair retry, timeout/cancellation handling, and stdout/stderr secret redaction.
Updated the Cargo workspace and lockfile for the new Rust crates and dependencies.
tdd_notes: Started with a failing core engine test for mock-provider output passing through repeated-prefix postprocessing, then implemented the minimal core path to green.
Added focused behavior tests for dyn provider use, VS Code and Obsidian-style mock requests, timeout, cancellation, and individual postprocessors.
Started pi-provider work with a failing fake-pi invocation/JSONL parsing test, then added repair, timeout, cancellation, and redaction tests without invoking real pi.
behavior_coverage: Provider abstraction accepts AutocompleteRequest values behind Arc<dyn CompletionProvider> and returns ProviderOutput or ProviderError.

AutocompleteEngine validates protocol requests, enforces request deadlines, observes CancellationToken cancellation, maps provider failures into protocol responses, and never returns provider text before postprocessing.
MockProvider returns deterministic insert_text for VS Code fixture requests and Obsidian-style Markdown requests.
Postprocessor tests cover markdown fences, repeated prefix removal through the engine path, max_chars truncation, explanation rejection, and newline normalization.

Pi-provider fake executable tests assert command args include --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --mode json, --thinking minimal, and stricter isolation flags --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates.

Pi-provider tests cover JSONL assistant-text extraction, malformed JSON repair with exactly one retry, timeout, cancellation, and redaction of bearer/API-key-like values and pi auth paths from diagnostics.

commands_run.result: passed after the initial RED compile failure and implementation
passed after correcting one test expectation for char-count truncation
passed after the initial RED unresolved-import failure and implementation

safety_notes: No real pi subprocess was invoked; all pi-provider tests use tempdir fake executables.
PiProvider uses std::process::Command/tokio::process::Command directly and only spawns the configured pi command; it does not expose file reads, shell tools, or workspace context loading.
Provider stdout/stderr diagnostics are redacted before being stored in ProviderError diagnostics.
Did not read prompt-buffer.md, tmp/, or unrelated ~/dev directories. A broad grep did return archive/ hits early in the session; no archive files were opened or edited.
known_limitations: Repair retry prompt includes the parse error and original request JSON, but not the raw malformed assistant text. This is sufficient for current fake-pi coverage; a future improvement could include bounded/redacted malformed text.

Subprocess cancellation kills the direct configured process and bounds pipe-drain waits after interruption; process-group cleanup for providers that spawn detached descendants is not implemented in this MVP slice.
The pi model remains configurable and defaults to openai/gpt-5.5; model availability was not runtime-verified here.
worktree_notes: Pre-existing unrelated deleted legacy reports/orchestration/* files and untracked orchestration/skill/archive paths were present before this task and were preserved.
This task intentionally modified only Cargo workspace files, new core/provider-pi crates, and this MVP-020 handoff.
next_recommended_task: MVP-021 independent verification of core/provider/postprocessing behavior.
